# License

**Proof-Physics** is released under a **dual-licensing model**:

1. **GNU General Public License v3.0** — for open-source / community use
2. **Commercial License** — for proprietary / closed-source applications

---

## Summary

| Aspect | Open Source (GPL v3.0) | Commercial |
|--------|------------------------|-----------|
| **Cost** | Free | Negotiated |
| **Source Code** | Must remain open | Can be closed |
| **Modifications** | Must be shared | Can be proprietary |
| **Use** | Any (commercial OK) | Proprietary / SaaS / Closed |
| **Distribution** | Must include GPL license | Unrestricted |
| **Support** | Community-based | Priority support |

---

## Open Source License: GNU GPL v3.0

This software is free software: you can redistribute it and/or modify it under the terms of the **GNU General Public License as published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the License, or (at your option) any later version.**

### You are Free to:

- ✓ **Use** — for any purpose (including commercial)
- ✓ **Study** — examine and understand the code
- ✓ **Modify** — improve and adapt the software
- ✓ **Share** — redistribute with attribution

### On the Condition That You:

- ⚠ **License** — maintain GPL v3.0 for derivative works
- ⚠ **Disclose** — make source code available to users
- ⚠ **Preserve** — include original license and copyright notice
- ⚠ **Document** — provide written notice of changes

### Full GPL v3.0 Text

For the complete legal text of the GNU General Public License v3.0, see:

📄 https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.en.html

## Commercial License

If you wish to use Proof-Physics in a **proprietary or closed-source application** without GPL v3.0 obligations, a **Commercial License is available.**

### Commercial License Grants:

- ✓ **Proprietary Use** — use in closed-source applications
- ✓ **Closed Distribution** — no source code disclosure required
- ✓ **Commercial Products** — create and sell products using the software
- ✓ **Multiple Seats** — use across your organization
- ✓ **Priority Support** — dedicated assistance and maintenance
- ✓ **Custom Development** — tailored implementations

### Commercial License Terms

**Licensor:** James Chapman  
**Software:** Proof-Physics (v1.0.2 and later)  
**Grant:** Perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive license to use for proprietary purposes  
**Restrictions:** GPL copyleft does not apply; closed-source use permitted

### Obtaining a Commercial License

To negotiate and obtain a Commercial License, please contact:

📧 **Email:** xhecarpenxer@gmail.com  
📋 **Subject:** "Commercial License — Proof-Physics"

**Include in your request:**
- Company name and website
- Intended use case
- Scale of deployment (number of users/servers)
- Timeline for implementation

**Typical Commercial License includes:**
- One-time perpetual license fee (negotiated per use case)
- All active versions during the license period
- Priority bug fixes and feature requests
- Optional training and consulting

---

## License Selection Guide

### Choose **GPL v3.0** if:

- ✓ Your project is open-source
- ✓ You want to contribute to the open-source community
- ✓ You're comfortable making modifications publicly available
- ✓ You want free software (no licensing fees)
- ✓ You're building internal tools or research projects

### Choose **Commercial License** if:

- ✓ Your project is proprietary or closed-source
- ✓ You want to keep modifications confidential
- ✓ You're building a SaaS product with Proof-Physics
- ✓ You're integrating into a commercial product
- ✓ You need dedicated support and customization

---

## License Compatibility

| Scenario | License | Note |
|----------|---------|------|
| Open-source project | GPL v3.0 | Free, share modifications |
| Internal tools | GPL v3.0 | Free, no distribution required |
| Commercial product (closed) | Commercial | Negotiated fee, no GPL restrictions |
| SaaS with modifications | Commercial | Required to avoid GPL copyleft |
| Consulting/Services | Either | Check your contract specifics |

## Enforcement

This dual-licensing structure is **non-negotiable**:

- GPL v3.0 is a **legal, enforceable license** recognized internationally
- Commercial license **requires explicit written agreement**
- Using Proof-Physics in proprietary code without a Commercial License violates GPL v3.0
- Violations may result in legal action or injunction

When in doubt, contact the author for clarification on your use case.

---

## FAQ

**Q: Can I use Proof-Physics in my commercial product?**  
A: Only with a Commercial License. Contact xhecarpenxer@gmail.com for negotiation.

**Q: Do I have to open-source modifications to use GPL v3.0?**  
A: Only if you distribute the modified software. Internal use is unrestricted.

**Q: What if my company has a "no GPL" policy?**  
A: You need a Commercial License. This arrangement is standard in open-source dual licensing.

**Q: Can I dual-license under MIT instead?**  
A: No. The licensor (James Chapman) controls the licensing terms. To use under different terms, you need a Commercial License negotiation.

**Q: Is this AGPL?**  
A: No. GPL v3.0 applies standard copyleft. SaaS providers should use the Commercial License.
